title: Weekly Strategic Priority Check-In
summary: A 15-minute Friday check-in to review progress against your quarterly strategic priorities, flag blockers, and set focus for next week.
inputs: Current quarter's 3 priorities and key results
Incoming strategic signals (customer feedback, competitive moves, market changes)

steps.title: Score each priority's progress
steps.description: For each of your 3 quarterly priorities, rate progress this week: On Track, At Risk, or Blocked. If At Risk or Blocked, write one sentence explaining why.
steps.tool: Notion, Google Doc, or the 90-Day Plan Canvas

steps.title: Identify and escalate blockers
steps.description: List anything blocking progress on a strategic priority. For each blocker: what is it, who can unblock it, what is the cost of leaving it unresolved for another week?

steps.title: Set next week's strategic focus
steps.description: Choose the single most important thing you will advance next week toward your strategic priorities. Write it down. This becomes your 'needle mover' for the week — the thing you protect from daily noise.

steps.title: Note any strategic signals
steps.description: Briefly capture anything you heard or observed this week that could affect your strategy: a competitor move, a customer insight, a market shift, a team constraint. Do not analyse — just capture. Review these signals monthly.

kpis.target: At least 2 of 3 priorities rated 'On Track' each week
No blocker unresolved for more than 2 consecutive weeks

whenToImplement: Immediately, even if you are a solo founder. The habit of weekly strategic reflection prevents drift. Takes 15 minutes every Friday.
delegationNotes: This is a founder ritual that should not be delegated. However, once you have a leadership team, each leader can run their own version for their domain and roll up to a joint weekly review.
automationNotes: Set a recurring calendar block every Friday at 4:00 PM. Use a template (Weekly Strategy Review template) to make it consistent. Over time, consider a dashboard that auto-populates KR progress so the check-in focuses on interpretation, not data gathering.
